idea.hook: A world with liquid lakes, rivers, and rain... but no water?
facts: Saturn's moon, Titan, is the only other body in our Solar System with stable surface liquids.
Its vast lakes, rivers, and seas are made of liquid methane and ethane, not water.
Temperatures average -179°C (-290°F), where water is rock-hard ice.
Titan has a complete 'methane cycle' of evaporation, cloud formation, and rain, just like Earth's water cycle!
idea.payoff: Titan boasts an alien hydrologic cycle, making it a chillingly familiar yet utterly foreign world right in our cosmic backyard!
idea.title: The Frozen Rivers of Titan! ❄️🪐

scenes.visual: Abstract animation of flowing blue/grey liquid and falling droplets on a dark, alien surface. Question mark symbol pulses.

scenes.text: Titan: ONLY other world with stable surface liquids!
scenes.visual: A breathtaking artistic rendering of Titan's vast, dark liquid lakes/seas stretching to a horizon under a hazy orange sky. Saturn is faintly visible in the distance.

scenes.text: Lakes of METHANE & ETHANE. Water is ROCK-HARD ICE!
scenes.visual: Split screen. Left: Rippling surface of a liquid methane lake. Right: Jagged, imposing mountains of rock-hard water ice, with a temperature overlay of -179°C.

scenes.text: Complete METHANE CYCLE: Evaporation, clouds, rain!
scenes.visual: Animated infographic illustrating Titan's methane cycle: liquid methane evaporating from a lake, forming orange/brown clouds, then falling as rain back into the lake.
A majestic, sweeping shot of Titan's alien landscape, emphasizing its scale and atmospheric glow. The view slowly pulls back to show a crescent Titan against the stars.
